key_fact: Damage propagation modeling for aircraft engine run-to-failure simulation (C-MAPSS / PHM08 challenge data).
notes: Same technical content as NASA_Saxena_2008_CMAPSS_DamagePropagation.pdf

key_fact: Faults / degradation are implanted by modifying rotating-module health parameters: flow modifiers and efficiency modifiers (and PR modifiers where applicable) on Fan LPC HPC HPT LPT.
notes: Official NASA mechanism — not OEM FMEA tables

key_fact: 1 operating condition; 1 fault mode = HPC Degradation.
notes: Primary seed for local FMEA / cases

key_fact: 6 operating conditions; 1 fault mode = HPC Degradation.
notes: Same fault as FD001 with multi-condition ops

key_fact: 1 operating condition; 2 fault modes = HPC Degradation + Fan Degradation.
notes: Use when fan channels Nf/NRf/BPR also drift

key_fact: 6 operating conditions; 2 fault modes = HPC Degradation + Fan Degradation.
notes: Multi-condition + dual-module degradation

key_fact: s1 / T2 — Total temperature at fan inlet.
notes: Often near-constant in FD001; weak solo symptom

key_fact: s2 / T24 — Total temperature at LPC outlet.
notes: Typically increases under gas-path degradation

key_fact: s3 / T30 — Total temperature at HPC outlet.
notes: High-confidence HPC degradation signature (increase)

key_fact: s4 / T50 — Total temperature at LPT outlet (EGT proxy).
notes: Classic EGT rise under HPC/turbine efficiency loss

key_fact: s5 / P2 — Pressure at fan inlet.
notes: Often weak/constant in FD001

key_fact: s6 / P15 — Total pressure in bypass duct.
notes: Often weak/constant in FD001

key_fact: s7 / P30 — Total pressure at HPC outlet.
notes: May decrease as compressor performance drops

key_fact: s8 / Nf — Physical fan speed.
notes: Sensitive under Fan Degradation (FD003/FD004)

key_fact: s9 / Nc — Physical core speed.
notes: Often increases when controller holds thrust

key_fact: s10 / epr — Engine pressure ratio (P50/P2).
notes: Pressure-ratio related; may be weak alone

key_fact: s11 / Ps30 — Static pressure at HPC outlet.
notes: Often rises with closed-loop fuel schedule compensation

key_fact: s12 / phi — Fuel flow to Ps30 ratio.
notes: High-confidence: more fuel needed as efficiency drops

key_fact: s13 / NRf — Corrected fan speed.
notes: Fan-module health tracker

key_fact: s14 / NRc — Corrected core speed.
notes: Tracks core speed correction under degradation

key_fact: s15 / BPR — Bypass ratio.
notes: Often decreases with fan flow loss / matching change

key_fact: s16 / farB — Burner fuel-air ratio.
notes: Often weak/constant in FD001

key_fact: s17 / htBleed — Bleed enthalpy.
notes: Common trending hot-section related channel

key_fact: s18 / Nf_dmd — Demanded fan speed.
notes: Command channel; usually weak as symptom alone

key_fact: s19 / PCNfR_dmd — Demanded corrected fan speed.
notes: Command channel; usually weak as symptom alone

key_fact: s20 / W31 — HPT coolant bleed.
notes: Cooling schedule often rises with hotter gas path

key_fact: s21 / W32 — LPT coolant bleed.
notes: Cooling schedule often rises with hotter gas path

key_fact: Damage is modeled as exponential deterioration trajectories on selected module flow and/or efficiency modifiers until failure.
notes: Basis for RUL / progressive symptom drift

key_fact: System health index H(t) uses operable margins (e.g. fan / HPC / HPT / EGT-related); failure when H(t) reaches 0.
notes: Links efficiency loss to EGT margin erosion

key_fact: Expected sensor directions for HPC degradation under thrust hold: s3↑ s4↑ s12↑ s9↑/s14↑; s7 may↓; s11/s17/s20/s21 often↑; s2↑ common.
notes: Aligns with knowledge/fmea/CMAPSS-Turbofan-fmea.csv

key_fact: Fan degradation adds / strengthens: s8↑/abnormal s13↑/abnormal s15↓; T50 (s4) may still↑ due to thrust-hold fuel increase.
notes: FD003/FD004 dual-fault signature

key_fact: Paper provides sensor response surfaces vs module flow & efficiency (e.g. EGT vs efficiency/flow). These are the physics basis for fault→sensor mapping.
notes: Strongest official NASA source for signature shape

key_fact: Use this CSV when NTRS PDF cannot be indexed. Prefer NASA Saxena Table-2 sensor ids s1..s21; do not use docs draft aliases s11=EGT/s14=FF/s15=PR.
notes: See knowledge/model_meta/cmapss-feature-definitions.json
